questions.explanation: Imperative sentence में verb से शुरू होता है। 'Open' base form है।
'Close' और 'Shut' दोनों same meaning रखते हैं।
Short, direct command — just verb + adverb.
'Go' is the base verb for जाना।
'On' is the preposition used with chair.
'Straight' is an adverb describing how to stand.
'The' is used as we know which specific book.
'Your' is possessive pronoun for second person.
'Look at' is a phrasal verb — always use 'at' here.
One-word imperative is complete on its own.
'Bring' means to carry something to the speaker.
'Take' means to carry something away from speaker.
'Put it here' — 'it' refers to an object.
'Move aside' is used in traffic or crowded places.
'Be careful' is a very common imperative.
'Come in' is phrasal verb for entering.
'Go out' is phrasal verb for exiting.
'Get up' used for waking or rising.
'Go to sleep' is common night command.

questions.hint: Do = करना, This work = यह काम

questions.explanation: 'Knock on the door' is also correct.
'Turn off' is phrasal verb for switching off.
'Fasten your seatbelt' is also very common.
'Sign here' is very common in offices.
'Send' can mean email or physical delivery.
'Put your hand up' is also common.

questions.hindi: उठो, स्कूल का वक्त हो गया।
questions.english: Get up, it's time for school.

questions.explanation: 'Turn off' is used for gas, lights, etc.
'Take out the trash' is more informal.
